No Poverty.reason: No explicit or inferable evidence in the course syllabus connects computer auditing to poverty eradication targets.

Zero Hunger.reason: No explicit or inferable evidence in the course syllabus relates computer auditing to ending hunger or improving food security.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: No explicit or inferable evidence in the course syllabus links computer auditing to health and well-being outcomes.

Quality Education.reason: The course explicitly provides professional accounting and IT skills (Target 4.4) and fosters understanding of sustainability issues (Target 4.7), directly contributing to quality education.

Gender Equality.reason: The syllabus mentions broad social responsibility, but lacks specific evidence or direct inference linking course content to gender equality targets.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No explicit or inferable evidence in the course syllabus connects computer auditing to clean water and sanitation.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No explicit or inferable evidence in the course syllabus links computer auditing to affordable and clean energy.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course enhances professional skills, employability, and economic productivity through technological upgrading in auditing and data analysis, directly supporting decent work and economic growth.

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course focuses on applying computer technology and IT tools for data analysis in auditing, directly enhancing technological capabilities and innovation within the industry (Target 9.5).

Reduced Inequalities.reason: Computer auditing skills can indirectly contribute to improved monitoring of financial institutions, potentially supporting market stability and accountability, aligning weakly with Target 10.5.

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: No explicit or inferable evidence in the course syllabus links computer auditing to sustainable cities and communities.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: The course develops the ability to identify sustainability issues, crucial for auditing corporate sustainability practices and reporting, directly supporting Target 12.6.

Climate Action.reason: Although 'sustainability issues' are mentioned, there is no specific evidence or direct inference linking the course content to climate action targets.

Life Below Water.reason: No explicit or inferable evidence in the course syllabus links computer auditing to life below water.

Life on Land.reason: No explicit or inferable evidence in the course syllabus links computer auditing to life on land.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: Computer auditing directly supports reducing corruption (Target 16.5) and developing effective, accountable, and transparent institutions (Target 16.6) by ensuring financial integrity and oversight.

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: Auditing skills, particularly in accounting, contribute to financial integrity and compliance, which indirectly supports effective domestic resource mobilization and tax collection (Target 17.1).
